post.content:
( Please correct me if i'm wrong/ there exist a better solution )

Q1 -  
observation - if arr = { 1, 3, 5, 6, 8 , 9 , 12, 15 , 17 } (sort if not already sorted ) , so the maximum sum would be for an arr = { 1 , 17, 3, 15 , 5, 12, 6 , 9, 8 } ... 
                       i.e - push 1 element  from beginning and other from end ... and increase,decrease respective pointers.
**EDIT** : 
BUT - one edit ....i.e place the last element(after all operations ) in front. i.e in the example - final_arr = { 8 ,1 , 17, 3, 15 , 5, 12, 6 , 9 } . since last element won't contribute much in the last since both pointers will eventually be side-by-side in the middle of arr.
also , this CAN be done in O(1 ) too ... since we are given 1- N integers...therefore , **sum = n ( n -1 ) / 2   - 1 + n / 2;**  { just take an example - which will bring us to the conclusion that n(n-1) /2 is the sum if we just did like obervation above ...and rest part will be the change/edit brought by shifting last element( which will be n/2 )  to the front.


Q2 - 
observation - I'm not sure but i think we can use Rabin Karp method and put their(each substring of strings) hash values in a set, and for each substring in a string , 
                       if (set.contains(substring) )  -- thats the first bad string for which condition fails.
Q1. n*(n-1)/2\nQ2. can be done using trie
